commit.message:
feat: DM feature parity — replies, reactions, delete; fix online status staleness

Backend:
- Add dm_messages.parent_id column and dm_message_reactions table (migration)
- Extend DmMessage model with parent/reaction fields
- Add DeleteDmMessage and ToggleDmReaction handlers and routes
- Add GetDmReactionsForMessages, DeleteDmMessage, ToggleDmReaction to repository
- CreateDmMessage/GetDmMessages now resolve parent author names and reactions
- Broadcast dm_message_delete and dm_reaction_add/remove via WS to both participants
- Sender now receives dm_message echo for multi-session sync
- Fix voice handler to use r.Context() instead of context.Background()

Frontend:
- DmMessage type gains parent/reaction fields
- dms.ts adds deleteDmMessage and toggleDmReaction API calls
- AppContext wires deleteDmMessage, applyDmReactionUpdate actions
- App.tsx: handleDmDelete, handleDmReact, onReply filled with author names
- App.tsx: dmAsMessages conversion includes all missing fields
- App.tsx: handlePresenceSnapshot replaces Set instead of merging (fixes stale online status)
- useWebSocket: handle dm_message_delete, dm_reaction_add, dm_reaction_remove events
- useWebSocket: remove 6 debug console.log else-branches
- MiniProfile: add hideRoles prop to suppress roles section in DM context
- ChatWindow: thread hideRoles through to inline MiniProfile
- reloadChannels: re-fetches channel list when current user's roles change

  - n_route: pure decision router (no wait, no actions)
  - n_one_store: uses first_id/first_mundep (no array access)
  - n_pick_search: calls ?pick=N endpoint (no array indexing)
  - n_pick_store: uses @results.busqueda2.id/mundep (flat fields)

commit.message:
feat: backup/restore via JSON + SAF (sem permissões extras) (#57)

* feat: backup/restore via JSON + SAF (sem permissões extras)

- BackupManager: exportar todos os dados (grupos, participantes, exclusões, desejos, sorteios) para JSON; importar com remapeamento de IDs e validação antes de limpar dados existentes
- BackupRepository: wrapper seguindo padrão do projeto
- SorteioDAO: novos métodos públicos inserirSorteio() e inserirPar() para suporte ao import
- ParticipanteDAO: novo método atualizarAmigoSorteado() para remapear FKs no import
- MySQLiteOpenHelper: DATABASE_VERSION_PUBLIC exposto como constante para validação de schema no backup
- GruposActivity: menu "Exportar Backup" e "Importar Backup" via ActivityResultContracts (SAF) — zero permissões adicionais no Manifest
- BackupManagerTest: 16 casos cobrindo exportação, importação, roundtrip, remapeamento de IDs e casos de erro

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: tornar importação de backup completamente atômica com transação SQLite

- BackupManager.importarDeJson: substituir operação não-atômica (limpar + inserir sem transação) por uma única transação SQLite — em caso de falha, rollback automático preserva os dados originais do usuário
- Deletar tabelas em ordem inversa das FKs (sorteio_par → sorteio → exclusao → desejo → participante → grupo) para evitar FOREIGN KEY constraint violation
- Corrigir estilo Kotlin: uma instrução por linha nos open()/close() de DAOs (conforme code review)
- BackupManagerTest: refatorar para usar semDaosAbertos{} helper que fecha/reabre DAOs em torno de operações de backup, evitando conflito de conexões SQLite no Robolectric

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: Prowlarr search + Transmission download integration, tagging pause/resume

- Search Prowlarr indexers from /torrents page, send results to Transmission
  with "indexxxer" label (separate from Radarr/Lidarr/etc.)
- Beat task polls Transmission every 60s, moves completed downloads to
  /media/xxx/Downloader/{performer_name}/, triggers scan/index
- Handles Prowlarr download URL → magnet redirect + .torrent file content
- torrent_downloads table tracks lifecycle: pending → downloading → moving → completed
- Sortable results table (seeders, leechers, size, age), per-row DL button state
- Add tagging pause/resume: Redis flag checked by nsfw_tag_task, re-queues with delay
- Stop now revokes in-flight GPU tasks (terminate=True) instead of only flushing queue
- Progress endpoint returns paused state, UI shows amber bar + Pause/Resume toggle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix AI Assistant modal shaking when content streams in (#47616)

* AI Client: Fix modal header spacing and make it sticky

The modal header used -32px negative margins that didn't match the
actual modal padding, causing misalignment. Update to -24px to match
the WordPress Modal component's content padding.

Replace the separate <hr> divider element with a border-bottom on the
header itself, and make the header sticky so it remains visible when
modal content scrolls.

Update negative margin and padding values from -32px to -24px in the
feedback suggestion and title optimization footer components to match
the corrected modal content padding from the ai-client package.

Auditoría ronda 4: estabilidad, overflow prevention, tests 94/94

Rust backend:
- u32→u64 overflow prevention en dedup_bboxes (área, intersección)
- u32→usize overflow prevention en mask_base allocation
- Bounds check en scores CLIP (vision.rs) antes de indexar
- VLM try_wait: detectar crash del proceso Python antes de enviar datos
- Two-pass dimensions: clamp f64 a [1, 65536] evitando truncación

Frontend:
- PixelSnapper: FileReader.onerror en drag&drop y file input
- PixelSnapper: try-catch en blob fetch y diálogo open()
- ErrorBoundary: límite de 3 reintentos, luego ofrece recargar app
- Logger: buffer de logs pendientes antes de que el backend esté listo
- GalleryCanvas: bulk delete secuencial (evita N llamadas IPC paralelas)
- PixelEditor: marching ants timer pausa cuando editor está oculto

Tests:

- drawPath con steps:3 para simular movimiento real de ratón
- 94 tests pasando (0 fallidos, 0 flaky)

commit.message:
Fix mobile submission, PDF generation, PWA static files, and upload limit

- Add PWA static files to git (service_worker.js, offline_queue.js, manifest.json)
  were 404 in production because gitignore blocked fleet/static/ entirely
- Fix gitignore: keep staticfiles/ ignored, allow fleet/static/ source files
- Raise DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE to 50MB so base64 photo payloads don't 400
- Add SITE_URL setting for absolute URLs in email notifications (PDF + report links)
- Add client-side photo compression (canvas 1200px/JPEG 0.72) to reduce payload size
- Unpin weasyprint version to get Python 3.14-compatible release
- Fix success page: remove references to non-existent engine_hours_start/_end fields
- Add horometer validation: final reading must be >= initial reading
- Shrink welcome screen "Iniciar Reporte" button to normal touch target size
- Add PDF download button to email notification template
- Fix send_report_notification to pass pdf_url and report_url context vars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use results.busqueda.* instead of webhook.json.* in subsequent nodes

@webhook.json does not persist beyond the node that calls the webhook.
Use results.busqueda.X to access JSON response fields in subsequent nodes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename ThompsonEngine to PathEngine

No Thompson construction left — the engine now uses recursive path
evaluation with direct index scans, not an NFA.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Klaviyo campaign creation — include message content inline

Klaviyo API requires campaign-messages at creation time, not as a
separate PATCH step. Reduces API calls from 3 to 2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enforce line-by-line translation via numbered line tags

Text instruction "keep each sentence on its own line" is unreliable —
GPT merges sentences when translating to Ukrainian (and other languages),
producing large unbroken paragraphs while the English stays per-sentence.

Fix: tag every non-empty input line with [001], [002], ... before sending
to GPT, and instruct it to translate each tag as exactly one output line.
Strip tags from the response. This makes line structure explicit data
instead of a formatting hint, which GPT respects reliably.

Result: translated text line count matches the original English exactly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix board layout: viewport-capped size, buyStock compact+expand mode

- Board max-width uses min(660px, viewport-height-based calc) so it never
  pushes hand/action panels off screen on any display

commit.message:
Replace NFA-driven BFS with recursive path evaluation (Karalis et al.)

Implement the paper's Algorithm 2 (EvalRPQ_TV) approach: decompose the
path expression into a tree (PathExpr) and evaluate recursively.
Transitive closures (Star/Plus) use BFS with a node-only visited set;
at each depth the closure body is evaluated as a recursive sub-query.
Non-closure operators (Concat, Union) decompose into sequential or
parallel sub-evaluations.

Key advantage over the previous NFA approach: the visited set is
HashSet<RawId> (just graph nodes) instead of HashSet<(RawId, Vec<u64>)>
(nodes × NFA state sets). No state-set explosion for complex regex.

Each Attr step is a direct PATCH index scan. Concat naturally composes
as sequential sub-evaluations. This is the "per-depth sub-join" the
paper describes, decomposed recursively rather than via GenericJoin —
equivalent for attribute-level path expressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
rebuild: car scroll animation with position:fixed overlay + lenis.scroll

Complete rewrite. Key changes:
- 300vh spacer section provides scroll space in document flow
- position:fixed overlay covers viewport — bypasses sticky/transform issues
- Uses lenis.scroll (Lenis internal value) instead of window.scrollY
- Uses getDocumentTop() (offsetTop traversal) instead of getBCR for section position
- Overlay shown/hidden based on lenis.scroll vs section bounds
- Header (z-100) stays above overlay (z-40)

Replace Convex preview seed with --preview-run action (#109)

* Replace Convex preview seed with --preview-run action

Replace the unsupported `convex import` approach with a programmatic `convex/seed.ts` action that runs automatically during preview deployment via `--preview-run "seed"`. Seeding is now idempotent and creates demo user, organization, pipeline, tags, accounts, contacts, and deals. Removes seed-snapshot.zip and export-seed.sh script.

* Add --preview-run flag and remove snapshot import step from workflow

Convex 1.32.0 deprecated --preview-name in favor of --preview-create.
